summary: Simplified the control flow of static item function it_802C0E48 in Dr. Mario pill item code by replacing repeated goto exit paths with structured else / else-if branches that preserve the same early returns and final false return.
what_changed: Updated src/melee/it/items/itdrmariopill.c in the static function it_802C0E48.
Removed the local exit label and multiple goto exit statements.
Nested the ftDr_Init_801498EC, owner comparison, scale update, and second it_802C0E48_sub check inside an else block instead of falling through via goto-based structure.
Converted the final outer conditional into an else if (it_802C0E48_sub(gobj)) path followed by the shared return false.
Kept the observable return behavior: early return true when it_802C0E48_sub succeeds, early return false after HSD_JObjSetScale when owner matches fobj, otherwise final return false.
smart_moves: Used structured C control flow to remove a label without changing the apparent branch outcomes.
Preserved early returns rather than forcing a single-exit style, which kept the function compact and close to the existing decompiled logic.
Avoided touching unrelated item logic, symbols, or data structures; the diff was limited to one hunk in itdrmariopill.c.
The cleanup makes the logical separation between the initial condition, the Dr. Mario owner/scale path, and the fallback it_802C0E48_sub check easier to read.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled function has repeated goto exit statements that all lead to return false, consider whether an else / else-if rewrite can express the same control flow more clearly.
Control-flow simplification can be valuable even without renaming functions or identifying new structs, especially when it removes artificial labels introduced during matching work.
Keep cleanup PRs narrow: a small, mechanically understandable branch rewrite is easier to review and less likely to disturb matching-sensitive code.
For item callbacks returning s32/bool-like values, preserve all early true/false return sites exactly unless there is strong evidence that the compiler output remains equivalent.
naming_conventions: Retained existing placeholder item function names such as it_802C0E48 and it_802C0E48_sub; this PR did not provide evidence for semantic renames.
Retained existing field names xDAC_itcmd_var0 and xDD4_itemVar.drmariopill.x4.
Used existing subsystem prefixes: it for item code, ftDr for Dr. Mario fighter code, ftLib for fighter library helpers, and HSD for HAL/SysDolphin JObj operations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced goto-based fallthrough with equivalent nested else structure, a common source-level tactic for simplifying matched decomp code without changing semantics.

Kept side-effect order intact: ftDr_Init_801498EC assignment occurs before fobj assignment, owner comparison, optional ftLib_800869D4 scale calculation, HSD_JObjSetScale, and the later it_802C0E48_sub call.
The diff suggests the matching-relevant behavior was preserved while removing an explicit label; no assembly excerpt or reviewer confirmation was provided in the slice.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only contained a cc to @lukechampine, with no additional rationale beyond the title.
follow_up_queries: Find other item functions with goto exit labels that only return false and may be safely rewritten as else-if control flow.
Check whether it_802C0E48 or it_802C0E48_sub later received semantic names in Dr. Mario pill item work.
Look for matching notes or map evidence around ftDr_Init_801498EC and xDD4_itemVar.drmariopill.x4 to clarify this pill-owner scaling path.
